evidence_snippets: not just COVID style, like all-hazards, even bioterror
run an investigation end to end, and monitor it
coverage_entries.reason: Stakeholders require end-to-end outbreak management including bioterrorism.

evidence_snippets: cases, contacts, exposures, that triangle
follow-up and progress for exposed/potentially exposed individuals
coverage_entries.reason: Discussion repeatedly cites capturing cases, contacts, investigations, exposures.

evidence_snippets: Every exposure record should say exposed to who or what and when
coverage_entries.reason: Explicit requirement that exposures be tied to who/what and when.

evidence_snippets: system should keep those traceable links
seeing connections among cases, exposures and the other stuff
coverage_entries.reason: Participants want traceable links to support source and transmission tracing.

evidence_snippets: Subject ID on every person
Race and ethnicity too, and country of citizenship
coverage_entries.reason: Transcript lists Subject ID and all named demographic and occupational fields.

evidence_snippets: capture investigator name, how to contact you
Name, email, phone, maybe team assignment
coverage_entries.reason: Team explicitly discusses storing investigator name, address and contact details.

evidence_snippets: Name, location, contact details for those
The hospital or clinic that reported the case, the lab that tested
coverage_entries.reason: Organizations (hospital, lab, employer) require name, location, contacts.

evidence_snippets: log a building, a floor, maybe a room, a store number
GPS coordinates help
coverage_entries.reason: Sources require granular location fields including building, room and GPS.

evidence_snippets: Required: species/type, age, gender
record tag identifiers
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes explicitly list animal fields, Subject ID, and tag IDs.

evidence_snippets: object name, type, physical descriptors
identification numbers like serial numbers, invoice numbers
coverage_entries.reason: Object data section lists names, types, descriptors, addresses, IDs and timestamps.

evidence_snippets: conveyance type (flight, train, bus, ship
If flight, store flight number and date.
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes explicitly require conveyance type and flight number.

evidence_snippets: capture at minimum time, location, nature
coverage_entries.reason: Notes state gatherings must record time, location, and nature.

evidence_snippets: Need a general entity for other living things
custom data collection questions for such entities
coverage_entries.reason: Sources require other-living-thing entity and customizable questions.

evidence_snippets: Capture an entity's travel relevant to investigation
Travel history as an activity linked to person
coverage_entries.reason: Travel history capture and linking to persons is specified.

evidence_snippets: Track suspected agent (pathogen/toxin)
suspected agent. Pathogen or toxin, or unknown
coverage_entries.reason: Agent (pathogen/toxin) tracking is explicitly required.

evidence_snippets: capture data for isolation and quarantine monitoring
Store start date, end date, instructions, check-in cadence
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes specify quarantine/isolation monitoring data elements.

evidence_snippets: follow-up and progress for exposed/potentially exposed
Periodic checks: logs of phone calls, in-person visits
coverage_entries.reason: Follow-up and monitoring of exposed subjects is described.

evidence_snippets: Questionnaire designer reuse
Accept digital signatures on electronic questionnaires
coverage_entries.reason: Sources require questionnaire design, custom fields, and digital signatures.

evidence_snippets: object type should be configurable list
Structured input and validation for common fields
coverage_entries.reason: Notes specify configurability and structured validation for data fields.

evidence_snippets: Name, email, phone, maybe team assignment.
mapping, data dictionaries, grouping observations by type
coverage_entries.reason: Sources call for personnel capture, roles, and entity classification.

evidence_snippets: assign a barcode at collection time
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes require specimen collection and barcoding support.

evidence_snippets: specimen and lab result capture (metadata, granularity)
use established vocab standards (LOINC, SNOMED)
coverage_entries.reason: Notes and emails call for lab result metadata, granularity and vocabularies.

evidence_snippets: fallback link to subject by Subject ID
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes explicitly define linkage rule for specimen vs subject.

evidence_snippets: inbound lab messaging and linkage to requests
coverage_entries.reason: Sources discuss receiving/parsing inbound lab messages and linking requests.

evidence_snippets: capture who ordered it, product name, date given, type, duration, dosage
capture adverse events post administration
coverage_entries.reason: Emails specify detailed countermeasure fields and AE capture for follow-up.

evidence_snippets: receive and process messages about administrations
Outbound: generating and sending a request message
coverage_entries.reason: Team explicitly requires inbound admin messages and outbound administration requests.

evidence_snippets: share counts like number of vaccinations
we owe DPH a plan for exchanging aggregate numbers
coverage_entries.reason: Emails and notes require aggregate counts exchange and bi-directional sharing.

evidence_snippets: mapping, data dictionaries, grouping observations by type
support HL7 v2 or FHIR for structured messages and CSV
coverage_entries.reason: Sources call for data dictionaries, mapping, grouped observations, and formats.

evidence_snippets: mobile app or handheld scanners (Luis notes bluetooth scanners in use)
coverage_entries.reason: Devices mentioned, but no explicit disconnected-sync or multi-mode deployment details.

evidence_snippets: share appropriately with emergency partners and the public, with approvals
coverage_entries.reason: Transcript requires packaging and sharing information with emergency partners and public.

evidence_snippets: all exchanged data must use secure mechanisms
secure transport rules
coverage_entries.reason: Sources repeatedly require secure transport for exchanged data.

evidence_snippets: stick to PHIN Cross-Functional specs
message construction and parsing
coverage_entries.reason: Email instructs implementers to follow PHIN message/transport specs.

evidence_snippets: Implementers must review those reqs
PHIN Cross-Functional specs
coverage_entries.reason: Texts explicitly say implementers should review PHIN cross-functional requirements.

evidence_snippets: use established vocab standards (LOINC, SNOMED)
configurable domain vocabularies
coverage_entries.reason: Sources require standard vocabularies and configurable domain vocabularies.

evidence_snippets: CRA functional requirements ... not optional
don’t invent our own AE fields
coverage_entries.reason: Email mandates conforming to PHIN Countermeasure/Response Administration requirements.
No references to system backup policies or backup procedures.

evidence_snippets: operational monitoring/alerts
filter who missed last check
coverage_entries.reason: Notes discuss operational monitoring, alerts, and detecting missed checks.
No continuity of operations or COOP/availability-after-catastrophe statements.

evidence_snippets: privacy/security considerations
No emailing PHI
coverage_entries.reason: Sources emphasize security, privacy, and protecting exchanged PHI from misuse.

evidence_snippets: No emailing PHI
protect exchanged data must use secure mechanisms
coverage_entries.reason: Texts require protecting sensitive information and applying privacy safeguards.

evidence_snippets: Implementers must review those reqs
coverage_entries.reason: No mention of providing employee training.

evidence_snippets: conform to the Countermeasure/Response
coverage_entries.reason: Emails require linking CRA process flows in design docs.

unsupported_implied.text: The system shall implement comprehensive operational and security audit logging (including hardening, persistent logs, and redundancy) to record system events, interface activity, sync/reconciliation actions, and security-relevant actions for operational troubleshooting and forensic review.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet:
Security and privacy:
- Protect systems and data supporting outbreak management from sabotage, unauthorized access, and corruption. Also protect components from failure. Raj notes hardening + logging + redundancy. [TBD specifics]
unsupported_implied.reason: The source explicitly calls out 'hardening + logging + redundancy' as a requirement for security and operations. While the gold set includes general protections, backups, COOP, and monitoring, it does not explicitly require persistent audit/logging of system and interface events (forensic/operational logs and redundancy of logs). This is a distinct, significant system capability implied by the source but not present in the listed gold requirements.
